title: PM Lane 204 - PM Intake Field Start Bring-Back Local Review Closeout Cue
summary: Added a browser-local `Local Review Closeout Cue` at the end of the existing field-start bring-back panel on `/pm-review/import-intake`.

Placed the cue after source review, customer/site clarification, lead/resource clarification, and later bounded packet candidate review are all visible so Jason gets a final no-write reminder before leaving the bring-back panel.

Explained that PM Lane 204 keeps the return as browser-local review only: context may be classified into source review, customer/site clarification, lead/resource clarification, or future packet classification, but no meeting note, task, owner, date, field direction, customer report, route, storage, export, control, or write path is created.

Kept the cue browser-local and non-interactive: no meeting-note capture, no localStorage or sessionStorage key, no export artifact, no backend route, no link, no button, no input, no textarea, no select, no task, no action item, no owner/due-date field, no lead selection, no crew assignment, no schedule/status write, no field instruction, no durable record, no production tracking row, no hosted write claim, and no production write.

Extended the focused read-only PM import-intake smoke to prove desktop presence, 390x844 mobile visibility, cue copy, zero links/buttons/inputs/textarea/select controls, no localStorage or sessionStorage local review closeout cue keys, no implied-authority link/control text, and zero mutation calls.
Used read-only sidecar Dewey for placement and guardrail review only; VS Code Codex retained PM lane implementation and final integration authority.

VS Code Codex instead kept the cue at the end of the full bring-back panel so the final no-write closeout reminder appears after source review, customer/site clarification, lead/resource clarification, and later bounded packet candidate review are all visible. VS Code Codex retained PM lane implementation/final integration authority. No files were edited, staged, or committed by the sidecar.
